text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 04 September 2023 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

William Harris, DOB: 15-02-1965, Hospital No: H563829 NHS No. 9273645810  
10 Meadow Park, London, EC1V 9BT 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior head injury in early adulthood/periodic occupational health monitoring for fitness to work/possible mild anxiety symptoms in busy environments/essential hypertension/seasonal allergic rhinitis. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.  
Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily.  
Ramipril 5 mg once daily.  
Cetirizine 10 mg once daily as needed.  
Vitamin D3 1000 IU once daily.  

I reviewed William Harris in clinic today. He attended alone and provided his personal seizure diary, which he cross-checks with brief summaries issued by his employer’s occupational health department. Those occupational health reports are provided approximately quarterly and have served as useful collateral sources when confirming dates and workplace events. He reports good adherence to medication with no adverse effects beyond occasional mild somnolence in the late afternoon. Sleep is generally 7–8 hours per night. He cycles to work three days per week and has reduced caffeine intake. 

Seizures: He and occupational health documentation both indicate stability over the past year. No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022. He describes rare brief episodes of momentary “zoning out” under stress without loss of awareness, injuries, or post-event confusion; these have not been observed by colleagues and have not impacted safety-critical tasks. There have been no convulsions, tongue biting, incontinence, or injuries reported. 

Work and function: He remains in full-time employment in an administrative role with periodic workstation breaks as advised by occupational health. No recent sickness absence related to seizures. Driving: not currently driving; status unchanged pending continued stability per licensing guidance as previously discussed. 

Examination: Alert and oriented, fluent speech. Cranial nerves intact. Power full in all limbs, tone normal, coordination intact. Gait normal. BP 128/78, HR 72 regular. No tremor or nystagmus. 

Investigations: Routine bloods from August 2023 (via GP) show normal FBC, U&E, LFTs, and vitamin D level at 58 nmol/L following supplementation. No new neuroimaging indicated at present based on stable clinical course. 

Plan:  
- Continue current antiepileptic regimen unchanged.  
- Reinforce regular sleep and stress-management strategies; patient finds brief breathing exercises helpful before meetings.  
- Continue collaboration with employer occupational health; their periodic summaries will be copied to this clinic record when available.  
- Safety counselling reviewed today; he is aware of general precautions and to seek urgent help after any prolonged event, cluster, or injury.  
- Follow-up in 9–12 months or earlier if breakthrough events occur; patient to bring seizure diary and any new occupational health reports to next appointment.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022.
No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure data: The letter states, "No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022." Clinic date is 04 September 2023. Calculate seizure-free duration: From 28 Jul 2022 to 04 Sep 2023 is approximately 13 months and 7 days (>6 months). Other episodes described: "rare brief episodes of momentary 'zoning out' under stress without loss of awareness" likely not counted as seizures and explicitly contrasted with complex partial seizures; also no convulsions or injuries. The clearest quantifiable seizure type mentioned is complex partial seizures, with none since 28 Jul 2022. Therefore, patient is seizure free for more than 6 months. Normalize to allowed format: "seizure free for 13 month".
seizure_frequency_number: No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022.
reference: No complex partial seizures since 28 Jul 2022
